title: Maverick Trading Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees

meta_description: Maverick Trading pricing in 2026: $2,500/mo, up to 80% profit split. See all plans, hidden fees, and how it compares to Apex, AquaFunded & Blue Guardian.
excerpt: See Maverick Trading's 2026 pricing: $2,500/mo, up to 80% split. Full breakdown of plans, hidden fees, and how it compares to other top prop firms.
content:
Table of Contents

  • Maverick Trading Overview
  • Maverick Trading Pricing 2026: Plans & Costs
  • Hidden Fees & Additional Costs
  • Profit Split & Payout Structure
  • Quick Comparison Table: Maverick Trading vs Competitors
  • Who Should Choose Maverick Trading?
  • Our Recommendation for 2026

Maverick Trading Overview

Maverick Trading is one of the oldest and most established proprietary trading firms in the United States, with a 25+ year track record. Unlike many newer prop firms that focus on rapid funding through trading challenges, Maverick Trading emphasizes a training-first model designed to build real trading skills. The firm specializes in US equities and options, making it a top choice for traders who want to master these markets with the backing of a reputable firm.

Key features include:

  • Structured mentorship and education programs
  • Up to 80% profit split for funded traders
  • Access to proprietary trading capital
  • Focus on long-term trader development

For a deep dive into the firm's strengths and weaknesses, check out our Maverick Trading review.

Maverick Trading Pricing Guide 2026: Plans & Costs

Understanding Maverick Trading's pricing is crucial before you apply. Unlike challenge-based prop firms, Maverick Trading charges a higher upfront fee, reflecting its comprehensive training and support. Here’s what you can expect in 2026:

  • Program Fee: $2,500 per month (as of 2026)
  • Initial Deposit: Typically $5,000 (refundable risk capital, not a fee)
  • Profit Split: Up to 80% to the trader
  • Account Size: Varies based on performance and progression

The $2,500/month program fee covers:

  • Comprehensive training modules (live and recorded)
  • One-on-one mentorship sessions
  • Access to proprietary trading technology and risk management tools
  • Ongoing support and performance reviews

While the upfront cost is higher than many competitors, the value comes from the depth of education and the potential for long-term growth. For traders who want to compare Maverick with other top prop firms, see our Apex Trader Funding vs Maverick Trading comparison.

Hidden Fees & Additional Costs

Transparency is key when evaluating any prop firm. Here’s what you need to know about potential hidden fees at Maverick Trading in 2026:

  • Technology/Platform Fees: Included in the monthly program fee; no surprise platform charges.
  • Data Fees: Most market data fees are covered, but advanced data packages may incur extra costs (typically $50-$100/month if needed).
  • Withdrawal Fees: Standard ACH or wire fees may apply when withdrawing profits, depending on your bank.
  • Performance Review Fees: None. All mentorship and performance reviews are included in the program fee.
  • Refund Policy: The initial deposit is refundable if you leave the program in good standing, but monthly program fees are not refundable.

Compared to challenge-based firms, Maverick’s fee structure is straightforward, with no surprise costs for training or platform access. For more on how Maverick stacks up against other firms, see our AquaFunded vs Maverick Trading comparison.

Profit Split & Payout Structure

Maverick Trading offers a competitive profit split, rewarding traders for consistent performance. Here’s how it works in 2026:

  • Profit Split: Up to 80% to the trader (based on account level and performance)
  • Payout Frequency: Monthly, with the option for more frequent withdrawals as you progress
  • Scaling: Traders who demonstrate strong risk management and profitability can access larger account sizes and higher profit splits over time

This structure is designed to incentivize long-term growth rather than short-term risk-taking. For more details on payout policies, read our full Maverick Trading review.

Quick Comparison Table: Maverick Trading vs Competitors

Prop Firm Rating Monthly Cost Profit Split Markets Best For
Maverick Trading 3.9/5 $2,500 Up to 80% US equities, options Beginner traders, options traders, education-focused
Apex Trader Funding 4.2/5 $167 Up to 90% Futures Futures traders, fast funding
AquaFunded 4.0/5 $299 Up to 85% Forex, indices, crypto Forex/CFD traders, global access
Blue Guardian 4.1/5 $249 Up to 85% Forex, indices Forex traders, challenge-based

Want to see more head-to-head details? Check out our Blue Guardian vs Maverick Trading comparison.

Who Should Choose Maverick Trading?

Maverick Trading is best suited for:

  • Beginner traders seeking structured mentorship and education
  • Options traders who want to specialize in US markets
  • Traders prioritizing skill development over rapid funding
  • Those looking for a long-term trading career with a reputable US-based firm

If you’re looking for the lowest upfront cost or want to trade forex, futures, or crypto, other firms may be a better fit. But if you value education and a proven track record, Maverick stands out. For more insights, read our in-depth Maverick Trading review.

Our Recommendation for 2026

Should you join Maverick Trading in 2026? If your goal is to build a sustainable trading career in US equities or options, and you value mentorship and skill-building, Maverick Trading is a top contender despite its higher upfront cost. The $2,500/month fee is a significant investment, but it includes comprehensive training, mentorship, and access to real trading capital with up to 80% profit split. There are no hidden platform or training fees, and the firm’s 25+ year reputation adds peace of mind.

However, if you want to trade other asset classes or prefer a challenge-based, lower-cost model, consider alternatives like Apex Trader Funding or AquaFunded. For those committed to mastering US options and equities with professional guidance, Maverick Trading remains one of the best choices for 2026.
